code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        # Remove surrounding quotes but keep numeric formatting intact
        return o.strip('"')

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The key is the predicate name; the value is a format string that can be
    # interpolated with the cleaned object via the placeholder {obj}.
    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "runway has a length of {obj}",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is located in the country of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj}",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has a first runway number {obj}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of {obj} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of {obj}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has a fifth runway number {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fifth runway surface of {obj}",
        # added common predicates for better phrasing
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "owner": "owner is {obj}",
        "headquarter": "headquarter is {obj}",
        # new natural language templates for taxonomy predicates
        "order": "is in the order {obj}",
        "class": "is in the class {obj}",
        "division": "is a {obj}",
        # Fallback generic template
        "default": "{predicate} {obj}"
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)
            # ----- New special predicates for more natural sentences -----
            if t.predicate == "language":
                sentences.append(f"The {obj} are spoken in {subject}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "officialLanguage":
                sentences.append(f"The official language of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "capital":
                sentences.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "currency":
                sentences.append(f"The currency of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "demonym":
                sentences.append(f"The demonym for {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "headquarter":
                sentences.append(f"The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}.")
                continue
            # Special handling for runway surface type predicates (e.g., 1stRunwaySurfaceType)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                # Extract ordinal prefix (e.g., "1st", "2nd", "3rd", "4th", "5th")
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwaySurfaceType", "")
                # Human‑readable phrase
                phrase = f"the surface of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj}"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            # Special handling for runway length predicates (e.g., 1stRunwayLengthFeet, 4thRunwayLengthFeet)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthFeet", "")
                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} feet"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthMetre", "")
                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} metres"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # If the template already includes the subject, render it directly
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    sentences.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj) + ".")
                else:
                    parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine remaining parts (those without full‑sentence templates) into one sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all generated sentences; if there is only one, return it without extra spaces
    return " ".join(sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        # Remove surrounding quotes but keep numeric formatting intact
        return o.strip('"')

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The key is the predicate name; the value is a format string that can be
    # interpolated with the cleaned object via the placeholder {obj}.
    templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "runway has a length of {obj}",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is located in the country of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {obj}",
        "runwayName": "has a runway named {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has a first runway number {obj}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of {obj} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of {obj}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has a fifth runway number {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fifth runway surface of {obj}",
        # added common predicates for better phrasing
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "owner": "owner is {obj}",
        "headquarter": "headquarter is {obj}",
        # Fallback generic template
        "default": "{predicate} {obj}"
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)
            # ----- New special predicates for more natural sentences -----
            if t.predicate == "language":
                sentences.append(f"The {obj} are spoken in {subject}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "officialLanguage":
                sentences.append(f"The official language of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "capital":
                sentences.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "currency":
                sentences.append(f"The currency of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "demonym":
                sentences.append(f"The demonym for {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "headquarter":
                sentences.append(f"The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}.")
                continue
            # Special handling for runway surface type predicates (e.g., 1stRunwaySurfaceType)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                # Extract ordinal prefix (e.g., "1st", "2nd", "3rd", "4th", "5th")
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwaySurfaceType", "")
                # Human‑readable phrase
                phrase = f"the surface of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj}"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            # Special handling for runway length predicates (e.g., 1stRunwayLengthFeet, 4thRunwayLengthFeet)
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):

                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} feet"
                parts.append(phrase)
                continue
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
                ordinal = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthMetre", "")
                phrase = f"the length of the {ordinal.lower()} runway is {obj} metres"
                parts.append(phrase)

            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # If the template already includes the subject, render it directly
